name: pr-review-workflow
purpose: Multi-round structured PR review workflow: Security Threat Model pre-check, parallel agent review dispatch, DAS framework (Describe-Assess-Suggest) analysis, learning capture to reviews.jsonl, deferred-item intake, and integration with Qodo + SonarCloud external reviewers.
external_refs: Gemini CLI (optional cross-model review — deferred in JASON-OS)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS v4.6-jasonos-v0.1 (2026-04-17). Port lineage documented in SKILL.md: 'SoNash pr-review v4.6 → JASON-OS v0.1 trimmed port'. JASON-OS reference/ has only PRE_CHECKS.md + PARALLEL_AGENT_STRATEGY.md (vs SoNash's 5 files — LEARNING_CAPTURE.md, SONARCLOUD_ENRICHMENT.md, TDMS_INTEGRATION.md dropped). Core DAS framework (Step 2), Security Threat Model (Step 0), and 8-step protocol are fully preserved. Data path: reviews.jsonl in SoNash has DUAL-PATH SPLIT (.claude/state/ for live writes; data/ecosystem-v2/ for migration pipeline). JASON-OS uses .claude/state/ path only. pr-review → pr-retro is a tight couple (pr-retro reads pr-review's reviews.jsonl output).

name: pr-retro-workflow
purpose: Cross-PR retrospective learning workflow: analyzes patterns across multiple PR reviews, promotes recurring findings to CODE_PATTERNS.md, generates fix template stubs, produces AI_REVIEW_LEARNINGS_LOG.md, and routes cross-PR learnings to TDMS and session-end learning loop.
external_refs: GitHub PR API (gh pr view for context)
notes: NOT PORTED to JASON-OS. pr-retro tightly couples to pr-review: reads reviews.jsonl output. The learning pipeline (promote-patterns → CODE_PATTERNS.md → generate-claude-antipatterns → CLAUDE.md Section 4) is the most sophisticated feedback loop in SoNash. This pipeline auto-updates CLAUDE.md Section 4 anti-patterns from pattern promotion — a critical capability for a self-improving system. Port requires: write-retro-record.ts + write-review-record.ts + render-reviews-to-md.ts (minimum). Full pipeline also needs promote-patterns.ts + generate-claude-antipatterns.ts. TDMS components (intake-pr-deferred.js) are blocking until TDMS is ported.

name: pre-commit-gate-workflow
purpose: Multi-wave git pre-commit quality gate: gitleaks secret detection (Wave 0), 14+ validation scripts across lint/doc/hook/agent/skill categories (Waves 1-3), nanosecond timing telemetry to hook-runs.jsonl, and HOOK_OUTPUT_LOG capture for CI visibility.
notes: JASON-OS has a minimal pre-commit (gitleaks + basic checks only, Wave 0-1 equivalent). SoNash pre-commit is 39KB with full Wave 3 (timing, telemetry, audit gate). Key structural differences: SoNash uses SKIP_CHECKS CSV variable (consolidated bypass), add_exit_trap pattern (prevents $? clobber), HOOK_OUTPUT_LOG redirect (.git/hook-output.log), and per-check nanosecond timing written to .claude/state/hook-runs.jsonl. scripts/config/hook-checks.json is the check-contract manifest (canonical list of all checks with IDs, commands, reads_from paths). Not present in JASON-OS.

fields: raw stderr/stdout of all wave checks

name: pre-push-gate-workflow
purpose: Multi-layer git pre-push quality gate: Wave 0 unacknowledged hook-warning escalation block, check-propagation registry validation, check-triggers security/skill gates, and a parallel Claude Code PreToolUse block-push-to-main layer preventing force-push to main.
sanitize_fields: check-triggers.js trigger categories (security_audit, skill_validation, review_sync — SoNash-specific thresholds)
hook-warnings.json escalation gate (requires hook warning infrastructure)
notes: JASON-OS has a simplified pre-push (gitleaks secondary check + basic guard only). SoNash pre-push is 33KB with full Wave 0 escalation gate and multi-check waves. TWO-LAYER enforcement: (1) .husky/pre-push fires on any CLI git push, (2) block-push-to-main.js PreToolUse hook fires when Claude uses Bash — both independently block. The escalation gate (Wave 0) reads hook-warnings.json and blocks if ANY unacknowledged error-severity warnings exist — the most critical behavioral guard in the repo. JASON-OS port of pre-push gate is partial (block-push-to-main.js is ported; .husky/pre-push lacks escalation gate and multi-check waves). Also requires .husky/post-commit warning-resolver pattern (SoNash: scripts/resolve-hook-warnings.js runs post-commit to auto-clear stale warnings).

name: session-begin-workflow
purpose: Session initialization workflow: duplicate detection, session counter increment, SESSION_CONTEXT.md load, branch validation, stale docs check, health script battery (8 scripts in SoNash), hook-warning gate, and goal selection — establishes all session-start invariants before work begins.
sanitize_fields: 8 SoNash health scripts (patterns:check, review:check, lessons:surface, session:gaps, roadmap:hygiene, reviews:lifecycle, hooks:analytics, run-github-health.js — all absent in JASON-OS)
session gap detection (Phase 2.4 — npm run session:gaps)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS v2.1 (SoNash at v2.0 — JASON-OS is AHEAD). Unusual version inversion: JASON-OS incremented version for bootstrap DEFERRED markers. JASON-OS LIVE phases: duplicate detection, session counter, SESSION_CONTEXT.md load, branch validation, stale docs check, hook-warnings-log.jsonl check (10+ threshold), goal selection. Entire Phase 3 (8 health scripts) is DEFERRED in JASON-OS. Phase 4 is mostly DEFERRED. The compact-restore.js hook fires on SessionStart/compact event and reads handoff.json — this is the implicit Phase 0 of every session-begin (pre-skill execution).

name: session-end-workflow
purpose: Session closure workflow: SESSION_CONTEXT.md update, plan file check, state file cleanup, metrics pipeline (reviews/TDMS/health — SoNash), learning loop, and final commit via session-end-commit.js — produces the committed session artifact and optionally runs TDMS + review consolidation.
sanitize_fields: Phase 3 (TDMS debt consolidation + metrics — blocked on TDMS pipeline)
run-ecosystem-health.js (health snapshot — 10 health checkers absent in JASON-OS)
agent-invocations.jsonl summary (Layer 2 hook output — absent in JASON-OS)
SESSION_HISTORY.md archival (3-session rolling window to docs/ — SoNash only)
D26 data flow steps (agent-invocations.jsonl, decisions.jsonl, commit-log.jsonl)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS v2.2-jasonos-v0.1 with substantial Phase 3 stripped. SoNash version is v2.2. JASON-OS port has explicit Lineage frontmatter: 'SoNash 41526 session-end v2.2 → JASON-OS v0.1 port'. LIVE in both: duplicate detection, session work review, SESSION_CONTEXT.md update (5-field contract per D12), plan file check, state file cleanup (Steps 8-10), pre-commit summary, final commit. STRIPPED in JASON-OS: entire Phase 3 (reviews:sync, ecosystem-health, TDMS consolidation/metrics), override audit, hook learning synthesizer, npm run hooks:health, commit analytics, SESSION_HISTORY.md archival. JASON-OS improvement over SoNash: session-end-commit.js uses process.execPath (not string 'node') and absolute path for log-override.js subprocess — more robust cross-platform execution.

fields: SESSION_CONTEXT.md regex dual-format (inline-bold AND heading format)

name: skill-audit-workflow
purpose: Behavioral quality audit of Claude Code skills: 12-category interactive walkthrough (v3.1 single mode in JASON-OS; v4.0 adds batch/multi modes in SoNash), mechanical self-audit via self-audit.js script, and findings routing to TDMS. Verifies skill adherence to SKILL_STANDARDS.md.
sanitize_fields: batch/multi mode (v4.0 additions — not in JASON-OS v3.1)
Phase 2.A cross-skill pattern detection (3+ skills threshold)
Parallel Phase 5 self-audit.js dispatch for multi mode
notes: PARTIALLY PORTED to JASON-OS v3.1; SoNash at v4.0 — significant functional gap. SoNash v4.0 added batch mode (produce all 12 category findings to tmp file), multi mode (audit multiple skills), Phase 2.A cross-skill pattern detection, and batched Phase 3 crosscheck. The LLM agent layer was REMOVED in v4.0 (D11 decision, Session #281) — self-audit.js is now deterministic grep+diff only. JASON-OS v3.1 lacks: mode=batch, mode=multi, cross-skill pattern detection, batched crosscheck, parallel self-audit.js dispatch. self-audit.js (scripts/skills/skill-audit/) is the load-bearing mechanical audit script — deterministic grep+diff verification of 12 skill quality categories.

name: skill-creator-workflow
purpose: 7-phase structured skill creation workflow: discovery Q&A, codebase scan, design decisions, scaffold via init_skill.py, content authoring per SKILL_STANDARDS.md, self-audit verification, and PR submission — produces a deployable skill directory with SKILL.md conforming to all standards.
sanitize_fields: EXTRACTIONS.md / extraction-journal.jsonl context scan (Phase 1 — SoNash CAS)
_shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md (present in SoNash, needs port or JASON-OS equivalent)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS v3.4 (current, matched). Both repos at v3.4. SoNash SKILL.md lacks the agentskills-v1 compatibility frontmatter that JASON-OS adds. v1.0 changelog note: 'Anthropic skill, Apache 2.0' — originated outside SoNash. The Python scaffold scripts (init_skill.py, package_skill.py, quick_validate.py) are generic. Explore agents dispatched in Phase 2 codebase scan (Complex tier); convergence-loop quick preset in Phase 5 codebase claims verify. _shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md is the production quality standard referenced by Phase 5 content checklist — this shared dependency must travel with any port of skill-creator.

name: statusline-tool-workflow
purpose: Go-based Claude Code session statusline displaying 3-line widget dashboard (identity/workspace, health/metrics, lifestyle/session) with API-backed weather, GitHub PR status, CI status, rate limit gauges, and hook health signals; builds to a versioned binary installed at ~/.claude/statusline/.
external_refs: GitHub API via gh CLI (H2 PR status, H3 CI status)
sanitize_fields: config.local.toml (gitignored, machine-local — weather API key + lat/lon)
6 dropped widgets in JASON-OS port (A6 active-agent, B3 worktree, D1 hook-health, D5 unacked-warnings, F15 uptime, I4 session-count)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS (16-widget port vs SoNash 22-widget). SoNash-ahead by 6 widgets: D1 (hook health via hook-runs.jsonl), D5 (unacked warnings via hook-warnings-log.jsonl), A6 (active agent), B3 (worktree), F15 (Windows uptime), I4 (session count). D1+D5 are highest-value back-port candidates once JASON-OS hook infrastructure generates hook-runs.jsonl. cache.go is the canonical source/runtime scope split example per SCHEMA_SPEC §5.3: source=universal, runtime=machine. CRITICAL: cache namespace collision risk — both SoNash and JASON-OS write to same ~/.claude/statusline/cache/ filenames (github-pr.json, github-ci.json) on same machine. Widget drop reason D1+D5 confirmed: requires hook-runs.jsonl and hook-warnings-log.jsonl (hook infrastructure not yet in JASON-OS at port time). config.local.toml is gitignored and not committed; contains OpenWeatherMap API key.

name: synthesize-analyze-recall-workflow
purpose: CAS synthesis and query layer: synthesize extracts structured insights from analysis.json files and writes EXTRACTIONS.md; analyze routes to synthesize (--synthesize flag) or recall (--query flag); recall queries the SQLite content-analysis.db index with structured search.
notes: NOT PORTED to JASON-OS. This is the query/synthesis layer of the CAS cluster — the downstream face of the 4 handler skills (repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis). analyze --synthesize delegates to synthesize; analyze --query delegates to recall. The synthesize + analyze + recall triangle cannot be ported without the handler skills AND the SQLite backend. Same all-or-nothing constraint as cas-pipeline-workflow (D21a). Distinct from cas-pipeline-workflow which covers the handler skills and ingestion scripts — this composite covers the synthesis and query layer. Together they represent the full CAS system. recall/self-audit.js is a scaffold (not yet wired, per D1d). The convergence-loop integration points are Phase 2.5 and 4.5 (quick preset) in synthesize.

name: test-suite-workflow
purpose: Protocol-driven UI testing orchestration: Smoke (all pages load), Feature Protocol (JSON protocol files from .claude/test-protocols/), Security (auth redirect, PII, headers), Performance (load time, page weight), and Report (JSONL + markdown) — using Playwright MCP or Chrome Extension as test runner.
notes: NOT PORTED to JASON-OS. Not portable as-is — SoNash product routes are embedded throughout. The protocol-driven architecture pattern (*.protocol.json files defining test flows) IS portable methodology. test-tracker.js (PostToolUse hook) writes .claude/state/test-runs.jsonl and is consumed by deploy-safeguard.js (test-pass gate before Firebase deploy). JASON-OS would need equivalent test-runner + test-protocols infrastructure first. The _base.protocol.json pattern (common assertions applied to all protocols) is valuable reusable methodology.

name: todo-workflow
purpose: Cross-session task management: CRUD operations on todos.jsonl via locked regression-guarded CLI (todos-cli.js), with JSONL mutation flow (7-step advisory lock + regression guard), session-begin integration for pending task visibility, and statusline E1 widget for current in-progress task.
sanitize_fields: write-invocation.ts invocation tracking (stripped in JASON-OS port)
notes: PORTED to JASON-OS v1.2 — FULLY IN SYNC (both at v1.2 with T30 fix). The T30 fix introduced the locked regression-guarded CLI for all JSONL mutations through todos-cli.js — 7-step mutation flow with advisory file lock. This is the only skill in the entire D1 scan at full version parity (not behind, not ahead). todos.jsonl is the persistent cross-session task store. Session-begin surfaces pending todos at session start. Statusline E1 widget reads ~/.claude/todos/ for the current in-progress task. JASON-OS port omits write-invocation.ts tracking (consistent with all JASON-OS ports).

name: warning-ack-lifecycle-workflow
purpose: Three-script hook warning acknowledgment state machine: append-hook-warning.js writes new warnings; resolve-hook-warnings.js marks individual warnings resolved; sync-warnings-ack.js flushes the lastCleared counter when all active warning types are per-acknowledged — preventing statusline stale unread-count display.
notes: PARTIALLY PORTED — append-hook-warning.js IS in JASON-OS; resolve-hook-warnings.js and sync-warnings-ack.js are NOT confirmed in JASON-OS. This trio works as a state machine: (1) append-hook-warning.js emits new warnings + log entries, (2) session-begin / /alerts writes per-type acks to acknowledged[type] in hook-warnings-ack.json, (3) sync-warnings-ack.js syncs: if every active warning type has a per-type ack, bumps lastCleared (which the statusline D5 widget uses for unread count). Without sync-warnings-ack.js, the statusline shows stale unread counts after the user acknowledges all warning types. This is a HIGH PRIORITY port for any JASON-OS project using the statusline. The trio is architecturally distinct from the pre-push gate warning check (covered in pre-push-gate-workflow) — this composite is about the acknowledgment state machine itself. D6d confirmed resolve-hook-warnings.js (D6c scope, position 57) is the resolver; sync-warnings-ack.js (D6d position 66) is the flush operation.

consumers: statusline D5 widget (lastCleared for unread count)

name: website-analysis-workflow
purpose: Creator-first website analysis with compliance pre-flight (robots.txt, Anthropic UA check), multi-mode operation (Page/Site/Expedition/Cross-site), superpowers-chrome as primary extractor with WebFetch fallback, and CAS integration via analysis.json + extraction-journal.jsonl.
sanitize_fields: superpowers-chrome MCP tool name (SoNash-installed — use WebFetch fallback in JASON-OS)
notes: NOT PORTED to JASON-OS. Part of the CAS handler quartet (all-or-nothing port bundle with repo-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis). The superpowers-chrome MCP is SoNash-specific but a WebFetch fallback path is documented — JASON-OS could port with fallback only. The robots.txt/Anthropic UA compliance pre-flight is a valuable portable pattern (ethical web scraping discipline). Has a code-reviewer agent dispatch in self-audit Phase 6 step 6 (creator-view.md) — the only CAS handler to explicitly name a code-reviewer dispatch.
